programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Margreeth start to research their new business idea, but can they find the suppliers to help them? Stephanie's big event - her singles night - arrives, while Sophie and James put on a walking tour near their chateau.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Angel Adoree advises Brits Jonathan and Michael on how to make their chateau wedding business work, before they try to persuade an American couple to book a big money wedding.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to floundering soul food restaurant Blackberry's in Plainfield, New Jersey, but struggles to get his message across to indomitable owner Shelley. Her refusal to deal with the truth comes to a head when Gordon finds a dead mouse in the doorway, prompting him to step up his efforts to change her ways.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in New Jersey to help Rose Leone, who spent two years in a coma and came out of hospital to learn her son Michael had run her once-thriving Italian restaurant into the ground. With a menu of more than 126 dishes, the quality of the food is suffering, and even the chefs refuse to eat their own creations.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: First of a two-part special in which Gordon Ramsay heads to the Burger Kitchen in Los Angeles, where tensions are running high because owner Alan Saffron has spent his son's entire inheritance on the struggling restaurant. To make matters worse, the kitchen is run by a chef called David Blaine who shares nothing of his famous namesake's magic when it comes to cooking - and whose threatening behaviour regularly gets him into trouble.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Part two of two. The chef continues his quest to transform the fortunes of the Burger Kitchen in Los Angeles, where tensions were running high because owner Alan Saffron had spent his son's entire inheritance on the struggling restaurant. Alan gets a wake-up call from internet critics, who he believes are trying to shut him down.(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on programmes including This Morning, Sunday's Ministerial Broadcast by the Prime Minister, Hospital: Fighting Covid-19, After Life, Paul Hollywood Eats Japan and Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?(n)

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Musician Jonathan is seeking a muse and spiritual healer, while 5ft 4 Reece finds his modest stature a stumbling block when dating, but connects with Chelsea over a shared love of gaming and issues with anxiety. Stacey is 33 and looking to settle down and sits down for dinner with medical salesman Paul, who is searching for someone to come home to after long days driving on the M62.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr invites Sean Lock and Kevin Bridges to take on guest captain Joe Wilkinson and Victoria Coren Mitchell in the words-and-numbers quiz. Doctor-turned-writer Adam Kay joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers.(n)

programme.title: Joe Lycett's Got Your Back
programme.desc: The new series continues as the customer service hero - and the man responsible for making complaining funny - fights the good fight on behalf of the British public, tackling viewers' consumer problems, no matter how big or small. Jess Phillips, the MP for Birmingham Yardley, helps Joe as he investigates budget flights and tracks down Airbnb scammers. Comedian Lucy Beaumont investigates how we can all reduce our carbon footprint, and Mark Silcox reveals some ingenious ways to save money on train travel.(n)

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul goes in search of some of Japan's strangest food stories on the final leg of his journey. He competes in a noodle eating challenge against two internet celebrities, who have acquired a cult following for their ability to consume vast amounts of food. He also pays a visit to a sumo wrestling stable, where he bakes flatbreads for the fighters and sits down to dinner with the largest fighter in the history of the sport. He then takes a trip into the countryside to visit a farm for very expensive strawberries, and concludes the trip with Osaka's eat-till-you-drop food tour.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Alex Sullivan hosts the first party in Chelmsford, Essex, hoping to impress with her birthday party-themed evening. However, an uncomfortable reunion between two former school friends gets the night off to a bad start, and a less than satisfactory dessert leaves the host with little to celebrate.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businessman Scott Johnson hosts an 'Essex Glam-themed' night for the second dinner party of the week in Chelmsford, Essex. His menu initially impresses his guests, until they realise he has taken some shortcuts with his preparations. However, a bigger problem emerges when former classmates John Mabey and Martin Young renew old hostilities, so Scott tries to lighten the mood by taking everyone for a dance in 'Essex's smallest disco' - his kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Deb Croghan hosts the third dinner party in Chelmsford, Essex, and hopes to impress her guests with a tarts and vicars-style evening. Following an uncomfortable game of guessing everyone's age, she perks up the evening with an unusual entertainment - a cucumber-based sport she claims is a traditional family pastime.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Twenty-five-year-old hot-tub salesman John Mabey hosts the fourth dinner party in Chelmsford, Essex, setting out to woo his guests with a Shakespearean evening featuring an Italian-style menu, but when his food comes close to a complete fiasco, he turns to theatrics to save the day - including asking his guests to act out Romeo and Juliet.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Martin Young hosts the final dinner party in Chelmsford, Essex, hoping to impress his guests by throwing a Caribbean-themed evening. The diners are pleasantly surprised by the quality of his food and shocked when he reveals a secret talent, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Rugby enthusiast Andy and dental nurse Holly kick off the competition in Yorkshire with a Mexican fiesta - a fun-filled evening, but with a few blunders in the kitchen. Day two sees married couple Mitesh and Jess cooking up an Indian-inspired meal, but with a couple of spice-fearing guests, they have a tough crowd to please. Finally, it's the turn of Yorkshire couple Paul and Mandy to host with some 'proper grub'. After two nights of Paul larking around he has a few surprises in and out of the kitchen to try to secure the £1,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from south Wales compete, including estate agents Trish and her partner Alan, who cook a glamorous fine-dining menu on the second night.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: The return of the programme property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a home abroad, beginning with Cath and her daughter Kate looking for a tranquil holiday home along the Costa Almeria in southern Spain. Cath wants enough space for her family to be able to visit, but she also wants somewhere to escape the rat race and find inspiration for her writing. Danni Menzies has five great properties lined up for her £170,000 budget, but will any be the oasis of calm that she has her heart set on?(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps a couple from the Wirral who are approaching retirement to find a holiday home in Tenerife's Costa Adeje on a budget of £150,000.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps pub landlady Alison search for a new home on the Costa Dorada in Spain, with a budget of £70,000.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman shows five properties to Pat and David, who have been married for 44 years and are selling up in Northampton to move to Portugal's Silver Coast.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas tries to find the perfect place for Tony and Carol, who have been married for 40 years and are looking for their dream holiday home in the Spanish town of Sucina.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to the Park's Edge restaurant in Atlanta, Georgia, where a series of spats with the local community are hampering its success. Owners Richard and Jorge's in-fighting has alienated locals, and the duo's suggestion that customer numbers are low because of racism has heightened tensions.(n)

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Love is in the air once more in Italy, as more hopeful singletons check in to the hotel, hoping to find their soulmate. Musician Jonathan is seeking a muse and spiritual healer, while 5ft 4 Reece finds his modest stature a stumbling block when dating, but connects with Chelsea over a shared love of gaming and issues with anxiety. Stacey is 33 and looking to settle down and sits down for dinner with medical salesman Paul, who is searching for someone to come home to after long days driving on the M62.(n)

programme.title: The Fantastical Factory of Curious Craft
programme.desc: Keith Lemon and Anna Richardson host this crafting series in which innovative creatives from across the UK use their artistic talent to produce spectacular makes.(n)

programme.title: Walking with Elephants
programme.desc: Explorer, conservationist, author and presenter Levison Wood treks alongside hundreds of elephants on their annual migration across Botswana.(n)

programme.title: The Plane Crash
programme.desc: Scientists, pilots and aviation experts participate in a staged aeroplane crash in a remote area of Mexican desert, hoping the exercise will reveal more about what happens to aircraft and their occupants during forced emergency landings. Pilot James 'JimBob' Slocum will put a Boeing 727 passenger jet on a crash course, parachuting out minutes before impact - with former US Navy pilot Leland 'Chip' Shanle then taking charge of the craft via remote control from a chase plane. After the crash, experts will analyse the site, along with data collected from cameras, instruments and hi-tech crash test dummies, to learn more about what happens when planes are forced out of the sky.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of a dual-purpose family and wheelchair-friendly home built by design engineer and former Paralympic swimmer, Mark Butler. Mark was paralysed from the waist down following a motorcycle accident and the home he currently lives in with his family makes it virtually impossible for him to complete even the simplest of tasks around the house. He and his wife Penny want to build a cutting-edge family home, with a drive-in basement, passenger lift and accessible kitchen and bathroom. However, building such a unique property proves a huge challenge, and the strain on Mark and Penny becomes almost unbearable.(n)

programme.title: Spring at Jimmy's Farm
programme.desc: A small camera crew follows Jimmy Doherty and his team during these difficult times as they 280 acres of land on his Suffolk farm and care for a wide variety of animals, from sheep and pigs to camels and meerkats.(n)

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast
programme.desc: Oscar-nominated actress Jessica Chastain dons an apron at Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty's Southend cafe. In honour of their Hollywood guest, the hosts pull out all the stops and create an all-vegan menu - including a cauliflower tempura summer roll. Jamie rustles up a colourful pea falafel fritter wrap, complete with home-made hummus, crispy chickpeas and epic garnishes, while Jimmy takes the idea of movie night to the next level by building a popcorn maker. The boys also report on the increasing numbers of people choosing to replace dairy milk with plant-based alternatives.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Billy and Gwendoline rope in Dick to help them put up a chandelier, and then they have to track down some wild boars that are causing chaos in and around the chateau. Clive and Tanith attempt to make a fully self-sufficient dish for their restaurant. Meanwhile at Paul and Karen's chateau, Paul continues the task of converting their water tower into an office - in 33-degree heat.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the Somerset House Hotel in Weymouth, Dorset, where Adam Borton and Holly Syme hope their boutique-style rooms and barbecue breakfast will impress.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Swansea, performing arts director Anthony plans a theatre-themed menu for his guests, Ross, Tula, Kola and Julie. However, straight-talking Tula tries to steal the show with a candid revelation and Julie brings the house down with some frisky fun at Kola's expense. Anthony's plans go awry in the kitchen, threatening to earn him more rotten earn tomatoes than rave reviews.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Portsmouth compete, with Claire and Alex kicking things off with an organic, nutritionally balanced meal that satisfies their new-age philosophies.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra complains she and Ray do not have any fun together - and persuades him to let her join him in his game of golf. Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton star.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Marie tries to gain the upper hand in her relationship with new daughter-in-law Amy, but Debra is determined the latest Barone wife will not endure the kind of interference she has had to put up with.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Seattle shrink's agent Bebe puts her lowly assistant in charge of his career when a popular new radio psychiatrist appears on the scene. Meanwhile, Martin seems unwilling to make a doctor's appointment. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Roz tries everyone's patience by talking incessantly about her new love Roger, and Frasier offers to help protect Martin from a bullying boss. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer travel to the Regency town of Cheltenham to help a single man spend £450,000 on his ideal house.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Lindsay and Paul have relocated 10 times in 10 years, so Sharon and Steve Garner try to help them finally put down roots by finding them the perfect home in Almería. Plus, Kath and Steve Cashley from Dundee seek a new permanent home in Alicante, on a budget of 200,000 euros.(n)

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: Following Brits working with animals in South Africa's national parks, with the first edition meeting two people volunteering at a sanctuary for orphaned rhinos. Cornish vet Emily attempts a life-threatening giraffe relocation, while marine biologist Alison finds a new way of dealing with plastic pollution on the Western Cape.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: First of a two-part special in which Gordon Ramsay heads to the Burger Kitchen in Los Angeles, where tensions are running high because owner Alan Saffron has spent his son's entire inheritance on the struggling restaurant. programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Countdown's Nick Hewer joins singer and TV personality Stacey Solomon, the Kaiser Chiefs' Ricky Wilson and Diversity dancer Perri Kiely for the latest culinary challenge. Presenters Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding guide them through the assorted tasks, and Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith pass judgment on their baking skills. On the menu are scones, a devilish technical, and a showstopper challenge in the form of self-portrait croquembouches, but which of the famous faces will impress those judges?(n)

programme.title: Walking with Elephants
programme.desc: Explorer, conservationist, author and presenter Levison Wood treks alongside hundreds of elephants on their annual migration across Botswana.(n)

With an escape and evasion exercise approaching, the recruits face a brutal set of tasks to build resilience; including a dangerous drown-proofing military exercise and punishing races across land and water. One of the strongest contenders suffers a major setback with an unexpected injury before the recruits must take up the challenge of evading a military-trained hunter force during a night on the run.(n)

programme.sub-title: The Case of the Deadly Toy

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team raids an illegal motor business run by US navy officers and find a marine's severed head in the boot of a car. Tony succeeds in tracking down the owner of the stolen vehicle with the help of McGee's hacking skills, while Abby tries to discover the identity of the dead man. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team suspects a desperate US Marine may have faked his own death with the help of a friend and blown up an ambulance in a botched attempt to cover all traces of the subterfuge. Subsequent developments, including a mysterious disappearance, seem to confirm the hypothesis - until a timely discovery by Abby throws new light on the case.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are called to a national forest where several women have gone missing and conclude there may be a serial killer at large - a theory confirmed in gruesome fashion as the investigation proceeds. Drama, with Mark Harmon and David McCallum.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to a high school on a marine base, where a pupil has taken his classmates hostage and is threatening to detonate a bomb strapped to his chest. Gibbs manages to enter the classroom in an attempt to dissuade the boy, but soon realises someone else may be controlling his actions. Drama, with Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is called to a frozen lake, where two children have discovered the body of a missing US Marine. The team searches the water for clues and finds three more bullet-ridden corpses, all of which have tattoos linking them to a notorious street gang. The case then becomes a race against time to discover what connects the murders before the serviceman's comrades seek revenge.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion attempts to save a man stuck on the edge of a massive sinkhole that threatens to permanently destroy the Los Angeles water supply. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A mission to locate sunken treasure in the Pacific goes dangerously awry when Walter and Paige's boat explodes, and they are stranded in the ocean - and surrounded by sharks.(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The body of a woman is linked to a serial killer who induces comas in his victims and imprisons them. Taylor and Flack's team must piece together the evidence with the help of a survivor who can communicate only by blinking. With Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes, Carmine Giovinazzo, Vanessa Ferlito and Eddie Cahill.(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Stella investigates a sexual assault in Central Park, a case hampered by the fact that the attacker left no DNA at the scene. Meanwhile, Mac and Aiden try to find the killer of a drug dealer - only to discover the fatal bullet has been swallowed by a rat. Starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes, Michele Hicks and Vanessa Ferlito.(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A practical joke turns into a murder mystery when a skeleton found on a tour bus turns out to be authentic. Mac and Stella are given the task of identifying the victim, but the evidence points to two periods in Times Square's history, 20 years apart. With Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes.(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Aiden try to solve the murder of a rap artist, who was stabbed after signing a recording contract and winning a DJ contest. Meanwhile, Stella and Danny search for the killer of a fashion designer found in her penthouse pool - a case that leads them to a sushi restaurant specialising in food served on nude models. With hip-hop star Master P.(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Danny investigate the case of a building worker who died in an underground explosion. Meanwhile, Stella and Aiden examine the death of a teenager found strangled and floating in a creek.(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A womanising patient disappears and Dr Sloan finds himself trying to untangle an ambitious murder plot. Crime drama, starring Dick Van Dyke.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Spanish Cross
programme.desc: Perry defends a young gardener on probation for theft when the boy is charged with stealing a valuable piece of antique jewellery and murdering its owner.(n)

programme.sub-title: The Case of the Dubious Bridegroom
programme.desc: Perry gets on the wrong side of a beautiful blonde private eye and a short time later finds his client charged with the murder of his wife.(n)

programme.sub-title: The Case of the Lame Canary
programme.desc: A surprising piece of evidence convinces Perry that his client is not guilty of murdering her husband, though proving it in court is a monumental task.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Vance's brother-in-law Michael Thomas is linked to the murder of a petty officer, and Gibbs is placed under pressure to clear the man's name. Meanwhile, a negative performance review leaves Tony feeling downhearted and he resolves to prove the assessment wrong by being less chatty while on duty. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine lieutenant's body falls from the sky and the team links the man's death to the disappearance of a Navy chaplain thought to have been kidnapped by rebels working with Colombian crime cartels. Ziva and Tony are dispatched to conduct a rescue operation and enlist the help of former Interpol agent Monique Lisson, while Gibbs discovers there was more to the chaplain's outreach mission than meets the eye. Crime drama, guest starring Karina Lombard (The L Word) and Jamie Lee Curtis (A Fish Called Wanda).(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A fire crew discovers a man's body after putting out a raging blaze at a warehouse belonging to a Navy-contracted electronics company, and Gibbs recognises the victim as Carter Plimpton - a former member of an NCIS black-ops unit. Meanwhile, a member of the Baltimore police department helping with the case appears to have a grudge against Tony.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's mission to track the arsonist targeting US Navy facilities appears to end in success when the man in question is killed by a thermite explosion on board the USS Brewer. Meanwhile, Abby studies evidence relating to fire-starter Bruce Johnson, who died when his car exploded, and discovers coded messages in his quarters that indicate he may have been working as part of a larger gang. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators discover probationary agent Ned Dorneget has been bugged by Harper Dearing, the man behind a spate of arson attacks on US Navy vessels. A phone call from the man himself makes it clear he is out for revenge, and Sam Ryan has information as to the motive behind it. Guest starring Richard Schiff (The West Wing).(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter and Sylvester go undercover on foreign soil, entering an international chess competition to extract a US spy from behind enemy lines before her identity is exposed.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: When a simple mission to protect a seed bank goes awry, team Scorpion need to protect Happy, Sylvester and Cabe when they begin to suffer from life-threatening hallucinations.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam and the team spring into action when fellow agent Dom activates a distress signal. They track down his car, which is riddled with bullet holes, and Eric intercepts a signal from his phone, tracing it to a house that contains nothing but some used medical supplies and a dead body. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell.(n)

Drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision: Come Together
programme.desc: With Eurovision 2020 unable to go ahead as planned, Graham Norton gives viewers the chance to vote for their all-time favourite Eurovision song from an eclectic shortlist, selected by a panel of experts. Graham also chats to James Newman, who was due to represent the UK in Rotterdam this year. There are previews of some of the other most talked-about acts who were due to take to the stage this year, and Eurovision fans and recreate classic moments in their homes. Former UK act Sonia even takes on the Epic Sax Guy solo that became so infamous after the 2010 Eurovision Song Contest.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision: Europe Shine a Light
programme.desc: A special programme honouring all 41 songs that would have competed in the 2020 Eurovision song contest. Just like the contest itself, the live show will be simultaneously broadcast across Europe and beyond, and will be hosted from the Netherlands by Dutch presenters Chantal Janzen, Edsilia Rombley and Jan Smit. There will be a virtual singalong, where fans from across the world will dress up and form a huge Eurovision choir to perform Johnny Logan's winning song for Ireland in 1980, What's Another Year. Graham Norton provides live commentary.(n)

programme.desc: A day trip in the air over the Dorset coast becomes a terrifying ordeal when a small plane loses engine power. Plus, two young lads begin to sink in the mud.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Charles Hanson help the teams look for items at the Gloucester Antiques Centre, while presenter Charlie Ross learns about the Birdlip Mirror.(n)

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: BBC London News; Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al's first day at Eve's takes a dramatic turn and old wounds resurface when a man discovers he may have a life-threatening genetic condition.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest becomes embroiled in an audacious art heist masterminded by his adversary Flambeau, who intends to use Father Brown and his friends to help him settle an old score. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A military couple seek a Shropshire home for their £500,000 budget, so Jonnie Irwin goes house hunting with the duo, and also meets a master bike frame builder.(n)

programme.title: Your Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman and the team give horticultural tips and advice to viewers, hoping to give them the inspiration to create their own summer gardens, beginning by showing how to make the most of colour.(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus Daily Update: BBC News Special
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: BBC London News; Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus Crisis: Europe's Migrant Camps - Panorama
programme.desc: Said Reza Adib investigates conditions inside Greek migrant camps, locked down as coronavirus spread across the world, with vulnerable people havinge to share taps and toilets with those who have tested positive for the virus. Strict quarantine is enforced on camps where coronavirus cases are confirmed, leading to some shortages in food, water and medical care inside. The programme hears from charity and public health experts who warn that any failure to control the spread of the virus inside the camps could lead to potentially dangerous outbreaks.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tina calls an ambulance for Jean and Suki steps in to help - but when she realises all is not as it seems, she tries to manipulate the situation to her favour. Callum and Kathy worry that Ben is unwell, putting his surgery in doubt, while Linda is taken aback when Phil seems more concerned about buying the Vic than his son's operation. Keegan's actions leave Tiffany torn, Isaac opens up to Denise and Ian plays Habiba at her own game.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Greg Davies and Guz Khan, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman and historian Lucy Worsley join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Connell bumps into Marianne back in Sligo and despite the awkwardness between them he offers to attend her father's anniversary mass the next day. Back in Dublin, the two study hard for the Trinity Scholarship exams, but Connell is mugged on a night out after the results are announced and he turns to Marianne for help. On arriving at her house, he sees that he has interrupted a get-together, which includes Jamie. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: It is the summer holidays and Connell arrives at Marianne's family house in Italy, but the obvious chemistry between him and Marianne causes friction with Jamie. Peggy cooks a lavish meal, but tensions run high during dinner, with Jamie drinking too much and picking a fight with Marianne.(n)

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: BBC London News; Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: People Just Do Nothing
programme.desc: The Kurupt FM boys deciding it is time to make a new track so they hire a studio and get to work, but it remains to be seen whether one hour will be enough time. Meanwhile, Miche searches for a wedding venue and Chabuddy G makes preparations for a date.(n)

programme.title: People Just Do Nothing
programme.desc: With Chabuddy G as their music mogul manager, Kurupt FM try to get a record deal. Meanwhile, Miche needs to pass a hairdressing test and decides to enlist the help of Steves, and Beats attends an Expectant Parents course.(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Jo Brand hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Katy Balls and Ivo Graham joining Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood show more clips from the natural world, including an alien-like snail with pulsating antennae and a 10-metre long animal called a pyrosome.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends visit a secret nuclear bunker in Essex.(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Joe Tracini and Meryl Fernandes enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Blue Peter: Meet Our New Presenter!
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall visits the southern states of America, where he spots a wild alligator and learns about the carnivorous Venus flytrap.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Florida to track down America's largest snake, catch a slippery amphibian called the amphiuma and witness the hunting methods of bottlenose dolphins.(n)

programme.title: Astronauts: Do You Have What It Takes?
programme.desc: The finalists go to the shuttle landing facility on Florida's Space Coast, where they experience weightlessness for the first time, have their motor skills put to the test at 30,000 feet and are confronted with the dangers of space travel. They also undergo further training at a NASA underwater facility before Chris Hadfield reveals who the panel have chosen as the winner.(n)

programme.title: Ice Age Giants
programme.desc: Alice Roberts examines the latest discoveries from the northern hemisphere to shed light on the lives and habitats of the creatures that dominated the planet 40,000 years ago. She begins by heading to North America, which was home to a diverse group of giant mammals, and discovers how the sabre-toothed cat's out-sized teeth were vital for hunting, yet easily broken. Alice looks at evidence of the bear-sized Shasta ground sloth in a cave in the Grand Canyon, and learns about the strange armadillo-like glyptodont - which was the size of a small car.(n)

programme.title: Frankenweenie (2012)
programme.desc: A lonely boy uses electricity to bring his dead dog back to life. However, the presence of the animal in the neighbourhood leads to panic over a monster, while the young scientist's classmates are soon also meddling with life and death to revive their own pets. Tim Burton's stop-motion animated fantasy, with the voices of Charlie Tahan and Winona Ryder.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the nine remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges for sportswear week. To test the sewers' ability to create practical and stylish sportswear, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a rugby shirt. In the transformation challenge, the contestants turn cagoules into miniature waterproof onesies for toddles, before taking on the difficult task of making tennis outfits in the made-to-measure challenge.(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall extensive wildlife tour continues as he visits Dorset, where the mild climate has provided all kinds of creatures with ideal habitat conditions. He meets retired policeman Mick Jenner, who has been filming an otter family for the past few years, and local bat enthusiast Colin Morris, who has been keeping a watchful eye on the rare Bechstein bat. Plus, at Portland Bird Observatory, Hugh joins ornithologist Martin Cade in monitoring migrating blackcaps and wheatears.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Looking at the Hollywood musical, with interviews from stars such as Fred Astaire and Julie Andrews, as well as discussion of films like Singin' in the Rain and The Sound of Music.(n)

programme.title: South Pacific (1958)
programme.desc: A naval nurse falls in love with a French plantation owner on an American-held island during the Second World War. However, dark secrets surface from his past and result in her having to leave the colony and embark on a perilous mission with the help of a US Navy officer. Romantic musical based on the Rodgers and Hammerstein Broadway show, with Mitzi Gaynor, Rossano Brazzi, John Kerr and Ray Walston.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram value some Spiritualist photographs and a Hornby train set at Culzean Castle, while Paul Martin follows in the footsteps of Robert Burns.(n)

programme.title: John Le Mesurier: It's All Been Rather Lovely
programme.desc: A portrait of the prolific actor's life and career, from his turbulent marriage to Hattie Jacques to his renowned role as Sgt Wilson in the TV sitcom Dad's Army and parts in more than 100 films. Featuring contributions by Michael Palin, Clive Dunn, Ian Lavender, Jimmy Perry and Joan Le Mesurier. Narrated by Julian Rhind-Tutt.(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Walmington-on-Sea leaves its mark on a visiting French general when Mainwaring's Home Guard heroes repaint Lady Maltby's Rolls-Royce just in time for an official engagement. Comedy first broadcast in 1974, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier.(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to Java's Central Province and the royal city of Yogyakarta, where he visits the Sultan's Palace and admires the ancient art of shadow puppetry. Moving north to Ambawara, Michael boards a scenic heritage line constructed by the Dutch with carriages dating from the time of his Bradshaw's guidebook. Moving on, he learns how coffee was first brought to Java for cultivation, visits the Great Mosque and ends at Surabaya, a port bursting with local traditions, produce and culture.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Dara Ó Briain, Zoe Lyons, Paul Chowdhry
programme.desc: Mock the Week host Dara O Briain comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo, treating the audience to his own brand of humour, before introducing routines by comedy circuit stars Zoe Lyons and Paul Chowdhry.(n)

programme.title: The A-Z of Eurovision
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal narrates this compilation of all things Eurovision, on the evening of what would have been the global singing contest's 2020 event in Rotterdam. The alphabetical guide recalls some of the greatest moments in the show's 64-year history - as well as those perhaps best forgotten. Highlights include the best interval acts, comic voting gaffes and the funniest commentary moments from Terry Wogan and Graham Norton - not to mention dancing gorillas, Polish butter churners, fainting backing singers and Celine Dion on a tractor.(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Steve Wright presents a selection of archive hits from the contest in this repeat from 2007. Featuring performances by Abba, Cliff Richard, Bucks Fizz, Katrina and the Waves, Lulu and Brotherhood of Man, plus an appearance by that year's UK entrants Scooch.(n)

programme.title: The Commune (2016)
programme.desc: A couple establish a commune in a large house in 1970s Copenhagen, encouraging anyone who wants to escape the outside world to join them and their daughter. A succession of unusual characters pass through the collective, but their supposed utopia looks set to be torn apart by an affair within the group. Drama, starring Ulrich Thomsen and Fares Fares. In Danish.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter H. Tony Allan and Giorgio Locatelli cook ham bone soup and Jamie Oliver makes a pine nut and honey tart.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is on her home patch in West Yorkshire, visiting Harewood House to learn more about its conservation projects and dropping in to her local horse and donkey sanctuary, Hope Pastures. Olympic medal-winning triathletes Jonny and Alistair Brownlee demonstrate how to keep fit under lockdown, Tom Heap discovers how school closures have hit vital outdoor education, and Adam Henson steps in to help a nanny goat in distress.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: From her garden at Glebe Cottage in Devon, Carol Klein goes back to the basics of propagating plants by demonstrating how to take cuttings. There is also an interview with Advolly Richmond, whose love of garden history is reflected in her own garden, and she shares her passion for a flower with a surprising past. Joe Swift has a large plant in his London garden that is due for some timely remedial work, and the team travels to Brighton to visit a garden packed with exotic plants grown in raised beds and containers.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Euan McIlwraith learns about a striking new discovery at the Calanais stone circle on Lewis and Anne Lundon finds out about the transformation of the River Kelvin. Arlene Stuart meets red kites and Dougie Vipond finds out how the fishing industry has been hit by lockdown.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson sows a rainbow veg plot in thanks to the NHS, while George Anderson is taking his recycling ideas ever higher by creating a cascade of colour with milk cartons.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Middle East
programme.desc: The writer embarks on a Middle Eastern food adventure starting in Lebanon, which exemplifies Levantine cuisine but is also influenced by its position on the Mediterranean. In Beirut, at the end of Ramadan, Nigel is invited to celebrate Eid with a local family, before travelling east to the Beqaa Valley close to Syria, and south to Nabatieh near Israel. Nigel explores the resilience of the country and its people through decades of war, and sees how this is reflected in their pride in their culture and food.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers share a few health-conscious recipes, including a wholesome soup, a low-calorie pudding and even a low-fat version of a family favourite - burger and fries.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following Baghani, a tigress chosen to breed in the wild. She is airlifted from her home in an Indian park to a new reserve where she encounters Rajore, a male who has also recently been released. The pair are filmed over two years, as they fight leopards for territory and learn to hunt boar, while trying to avoid being killed by poachers.(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: What people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the second episode, a Shinto devotee undertakes a gruelling challenge at the sacred Nachi Falls in Japan, hoping to ensure the wellbeing of his country for another year. Meanwhile in Mali, a young Muslim man helps to re-plaster Djenne's Great Mosque in the hope of a place in paradise, and at the Cathedral of St John the Divine in New York, an Anglican priest manages an astonishing ceremony to bring people - and their animals - closer to God.(n)

programme.title: Paint Your Wagon (1969)
programme.desc: A gold prospector buys a wife from a Mormon and agrees to share her with his mining partner when she insists they all live together. Mindful that her presence in an all-male town might give rise to jealousy, he hijacks a stagecoach carrying six working girls and opens a brothel in town. Oscar-nominated musical Western, starring Lee Marvin, Clint Eastwood, with Jean Seberg and Harve Presnell.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction.(n)

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part one of two. The reasons why young animals enjoy playing, and how it can teach them to survive in the wild. The programme explores how young elephants, which rarely leave their mothers' side for the first eight years of life, play to develop social skills, while macaques hone their mental agility by playing games with stones. Plus, a zoologist who looks after orphaned cheetahs reveals how play can help cubs learn to hunt.(n)

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The final leg of the journey takes in Morocco, where Simon visits a land borders between Africa and the European Union, and the coast of Spain, taking in the scale of the country's fruit and vegetable industry. He discovers why tourists are actively discouraged from visiting Corsica, and goes diving in waters that have the highest fish population in the Mediterranean.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani consider ideas from a pair of porridge-makers with a teddy bears' picnic-themed breakfast, two students who have some new ideas for an established marketplace, an Irish inventor who made a breakthrough with the help of his father, and an entrepreneur with a new approach to weight loss involving wearing more clothes.(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: Ally tries to open Dave up to new sexual experiences, but inadvertently exposes his deepest, darkest insecurities. Sitcom about a neurotic suburbanite who is convinced he is destined to be a rap star.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: Mike decides he should become Dave's manager, and Dave is convinced he has picked up an infectious illness from rapper Young Thug at a party. Elz gets to work with Trippie Redd.(n)

programme.title: Charlie Brooker's Antiviral Wipe
programme.desc: The writer and broadcaster returns to front his own show since the award-winning Charlie Brooker's 2016 Wipe, to take a satirical look at life under lockdown. As well as the crisis itself, he will be finding out what people have been watching to while away the hours, with help from the ever-insightful Philomena Cunk.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: Tensions run high as the housemates prepare to host the Biannual Vampire Orgy - and Guillermo fears that an old friend of his will end up being killed during the party. Comedy, starring Matt Berry, Kayvan Novak, and Natasia Demetriou.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: Nandor pays a visit to one of his living descendants, while Nadja is reunited with her continually reincarnated lover Gregor, still in the guide of nightwatchman Jeff. Comedy, starring Matt Berry, Kayvan Novak, and Natasia Demetriou.(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week.(n)

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Istanbul
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles, Edwina Currie, Fatima Whitbread, Mim Shaikh, Amar Latif, Dom Joly and Pauline McLynn still have 650km to travel to reach Istanbul. The last leg of their route takes them through more Bulgarian forest in the Rhodope Mountains, involving a tricky 1,500m climb. Near the summit, the incline increases, though the breath-taking view proves worth it. In Batak, they visit the small orthodox church of St Nedelya where around 5,000 people were massacred by Ottoman militia in the 19th century. Eventually, they arrive in Turkey, heading for the magnificent Suleymaniye Mosque in Istanbul.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: A 22-year-old father-to-be must face his fear of surgery when his partner needs a caesarean section after the baby's heart rate starts to dip. A mother of four boys prepares to give birth to her fifth child, and being an old hand at delivery, soon has a new addition to her brood with no complications. A high-risk pregnancy is closely monitored, and a woman who has been in the hospital for a week is still waiting for the birth of her first baby to begin - but she and her partner are prepared to work as a team when the time comes.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents from Brighton Racecourse, with experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey helping the teams. Charlie Ross also visits the National Horseracing Museum.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Ellesmere Port, where Rachael, Lyndsey and daughter Missy-Mae want a garden to match their fun-loving personalities.(n)

programme.title: Primates
programme.desc: Chris Packham narrates a look at the complex social and family lives of the animal group, including leaf monkeys competing to babysit a bright orange infant and rally to defend it from a python, and gibbons learning daredevil treetop acrobatics with their playmates. In the Amazon, a female spider monkey elder leads its troop to aquatic plants brought within reach on raging floodwaters and shows them how they can feast on this unlikely bounty by using their remarkable tails as safety ropes.(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, with David Cornock.(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The last quarter-final sees amateur designers transforming two rooms in 1930s art deco flats. One hopeful takes a risk by introducing a 'mock crocodile' pattern to their client's gentlemen's club brief, while the other's butterfly-inspired effect seems a little too dominant. However, the challenge is really on for one designer when a possible asbestos outbreak means work has to be halted. Will they get finished on time? Presented by Tom Dyckhoff, with judges Kelly Hoppen and Daniel Hopwood.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash. As the teams solve the passwords, they win letters that will help them later in the game, giving them a better chance of cracking the final password which unlocks the Letterbox holding the cash prize. However, if they end up as the losing pair along the way, they'll have to surrender all the letters they've won to the winning pair - who will then go through to the semi-final eliminator round.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team explores some of western Europe's most dramatic shorelines, telling stories, investigating notable landmarks and exploring natural enigmas that have played an important role in the region's development. This edition focuses on the mysteries of the British Isles.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms narrates a history of Westerns, featuring archive interviews with stars including Gary Cooper, James Stewart, Yul Brynner, Paul Newman, John Wayne and Clint Eastwood.(n)

programme.title: Wagon Master (1950)
programme.desc: Two itinerant cowboys help guide a Mormon wagon train on its dangerous journey west in search of the promised land, crossing paths with travelling entertainers, rowdy gunfighters, cut-throat natives and a fugitive band of ruthless outlaws. John Ford's Western, starring Ben Johnson, Joanne Dru, Ward Bond, Harry Carey Jr and Charles Kemper.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Claire Rawle and Jonathan Pratt assess items brought to the RAF Museum in Cosford, Shropshire, while Paul Martin finds out about saddlemaking in Dudley.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu, Charlie Higson, Kate Williams and Tom Allen go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Heroes
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble celebrate the stars of Longleat, beginning by meeting the dedicated keepers who have been rehabilitating Asian elephant Annie - a former circus animal that was abused and mistreated.(n)

programme.title: Dig WW2 with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian explores Northern Ireland's role in the Second World War, revealing the story of a pilot who became the most successful U-boat hunter of all time. The programme also examines Harland & Wolff's contributions to the development of the famous Churchill tank, and takes an underwater look at the wreck of the U-155. In Donegal, a major excavation reveals the 70-year-old Spitfire flown by American pilot Bud Wolfe, who was forced to bail out just minutes after taking off.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Joe Swift take a look back over the last decade of world-class design and horticulture from the RHS Chelsea Flower Show from their own gardens, beginning by celebrating the very best of British. From roses to royalty, they reveal more about the people and plants that fuel the nation's obsession with gardening, and meet the designers and the passionate people inspired by the UK's rich history and landscape.(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Adventures
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull travel around Britain as they explore their favourite scientific breakthroughs, beginning with the 18th-century inventions that kick-started the Industrial Revolution and transformed scientific understanding. They head to Derbyshire to investigate Richard Arkwright's water frame - a spinning machine that allowed cotton textiles to be mass-produced, and then examine James Watt's separate condenser, an unassuming invention that would be the biggest single improvement ever made to the steam engine.(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Bill Bailey, Colin Lane and Desiree Burch join regular panellist Alan Davies to answer host Sandi Toksvig's questions on the theme 'Overseas', in an extended edition of the unorthodox quiz. During the course of the show, the panellists find out about Allotment Annies as well as who keeps their brothers in a cage. The fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers the questionmaster considers most interesting, though not necessarily correct.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Being Gail Porter
programme.desc: For the first time, Gail Porter opens up about her past, hoping to understand her rise to celebrity and her fall into depression, anorexia, self-harming and homelessness.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: Natural World: Pygmy Hippo - A Very Secret Life
programme.desc: Solitary, secretive and extremely hard to find, the pygmy hippo is one of nature's last great mysteries and scientists know almost nothing about its life in the wild and what it needs to survive. This documentary follows Australian ecologist Wei-Yeen Yap as she heads to a remote part of the West African rainforest to investigate its life and habits, hoping to gain knowledge that will help save the animal from extinction. David Harewood narrates.(n)

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: How the landscapes of America's five great deserts challenged the pioneers' westward push. Beginning in Monument Valley on the Arizona-Utah state line, Ray discovers how the US cavalry struggled in the hot arid landscape, explores how the Apache adapted their warfare methods to the hostile conditions and reveals why the forbidding landscape proved the perfect refuge for bandits.(n)

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Phillips Petroleum is looking for a place by the coast to build a gigantic tank for oil storage and Dirdal Valley, where Anna's family's farm is situated, is one of the areas they are considering. What Anna learns from her work at the mayor's office has dire consequences. In Norwegian and English.(n)

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Christian and Martin start their own diving company and are in need of start-up capital, while Ingrid and Fredrik Nyman make a plan to help their son, and an old friend of Toril's returns to town. In Norwegian and English.(n)

programme.title: Made in Dagenham (2010)
programme.desc: The female employees making upholstery at Ford's London plant in the 1960s campaign for the right to equal pay, only for the company to declare their work is unskilled. The women embark on a national protest, taking their grievance all the way to the government and demanding a change in the law. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Sally Hawkins, Miranda Richardson and Bob Hoskins.(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances by some of the most popular solo artists to have appeared on the show, including songs by Cliff Richard, Madonna, Marc Bolan, David Cassidy, Justin Bieber, Rihanna and other well-received pop pin-ups.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell and Anthea Turner present the edition originally broadcast on 17 August, featuring performances by acts including Neneh Cherry, Martika and Fuzzbox.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Jakki Brambles present the edition first shown on 24 August, featuring performances by the likes of Then Jerico, Cliff Richard and Bon Jovi.(n)

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: How the landscapes of America's five great deserts challenged the pioneers' westward push. Beginning in Monument Valley on the Arizona-Utah state line, Ray discovers how the US cavalry struggled in the hot arid landscape, explores how the Apache adapted their warfare methods to the hostile conditions and reveals why the forbidding landscape proved the perfect refuge for bandits.(n)

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: Two French Horn players, two trombonists and a tuba player compete in the brass category final of BBC Young Musician 2020, for a place in the competition's semi-finals. Anna Lapwood presents highlights of the competition, filmed at the Royal Welsh College of Music and Drama in Cardiff, and is joined by one of the world's leading euphonium players, David Childs, to review each performance.(n)

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.sub-title: New Forest - A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: Exploring 12 months in the lives of people and wildlife inhabiting the New Forest National Park in Hampshire. Although only created in 2005, its woodlands are among the oldest in the country and have supported lifestyles dating back to medieval times. Residents including coppicer Dave Dibden show how their traditions have continued into the present day, and the film also features footage of some of Britain's rarest creatures, including sand lizards, Dartford warblers and fellow birds hobbies. Narrated by Hermione Norris.(n)

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: How variations on the theme of family are depicted in Australian movie-making The Castle's nuclear, if unorthodox family, a family of faith in The Devil's Playground, Romper Stomper's frightening Neo-Nazis and crime families such as those depicted in Ned Kelly and Animal Kingdom.(n)

programme.title: Retreat: Meditations from a Monastery
programme.desc: Life in Belmont Abbey, a Benedictine monastery in Herefordshire, where monks including a renowned iconographer and another who creates manuscripts and rosaries.(n)

programme.title: Performance Live
programme.desc: A surreal, theatrical adventure in which a young person escapes into a seemingly empty building at night, and meets a mysterious guide who offers them an alternative way out. Filmed in Battersea Arts Centre, in one continuous, unbroken shot, this is an immersive journey through a labyrinth of rooms and corridors, propelled by performances from extraordinary artists. Omid Djalili leads the journey as the enigmatic Guide.(n)

programme.title: Tom Waits: Tales from a Cracked Jukebox
programme.desc: Profile of the influential and inventive singer-songwriter, whose work encompasses a mixture of musical styles, from blues to Weimar cabaret. The programme incorporates rare archive recordings and interviews, and features contributions from Terry Gilliam, Ian Rankin and Ute Lemper.(n)

programme.title: Oceans Apart: Art and the Pacific with James Fox
programme.desc: Continuing his exploration of the collision of the West and Pacific culture, art historian Dr James Fox looks at how the West has created a vision of Polynesia as paradise. He travels across the Pacific to uncover the great sites and masterpieces of pre-contact Polynesian art, from the massive religious complex Taputapuatea on the island of Raiatea to the extraordinary feathered Ku heads from Hawaii, testament to the rich and sophisticated societies that once lived there.(n)

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: Two French Horn players, two trombonists and a tuba player compete in the brass category final of BBC Young Musician 2020, for a place in the competition's semi-finals. Anna Lapwood presents highlights of the competition, filmed at the Royal Welsh College of Music and Drama in Cardiff, and is joined by one of the world's leading euphonium players, David Childs, to review each performance.(n)

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.sub-title: New Forest - A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: Exploring 12 months in the lives of people and wildlife inhabiting the New Forest National Park in Hampshire. Although only created in 2005, its woodlands are among the oldest in the country and have supported lifestyles dating back to medieval times. Residents including coppicer Dave Dibden show how their traditions have continued into the present day, and the film also features footage of some of Britain's rarest creatures, including sand lizards, Dartford warblers and fellow birds hobbies. Narrated by Hermione Norris.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that shows even a tiny shanty by a pond can be a warm place in the dead of winter.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Biologist Ben Garrod examines the evolution of skin - the largest organ in any creature's body - across all species of the animal kingdom, beginning by looking at how that of vertebrates has allowed them to conquer the planet.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr's History of Modern Britain
programme.desc: The journalist considers whether the often-accepted view that Britain entered a golden age in the 1950s glosses over the social and racial tensions of the decade. He also examines how the new breed of 1960s satirists taught the public to laugh at their rulers and how factions including the Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament began to protest against those in power.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Tom Baker narrates a programme examining the evolution of the loaf in Britain and how the bread that people consumed in the past had a lot to say about their social status. From the time when white bread was exclusively for the rich, to the moment when dietary experts recommended brown loaves for better health, this documentary explains how habits in buying and eating bread have changed.(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: The Changin' Times of Ike White - Arena
programme.desc: Documentary in which Dan Vernon tries to track down Ike White, a musical prodigy who was serving life in prison for murder when he was given the opportunity to record an album. The resulting record Changin' Times came out in 1976 and following his release from prison, Whiteseemed to be on the path to redemption, but just as he was charting a course to stardom, he disappeared.(n)

programme.title: Morocco to Timbuktu: An Arabian Adventure
programme.desc: Writer and explorer Alice Morrison embarks on a camel trek over the dunes of the Sahara, 3.5 million square miles of desert, for the second and final part of her journey to Timbuktu. Along the way she visits a livestock market that centuries ago provided merchants with their camels, before reaching her final destination. Far from the legendary City of Gold, Timbuktu is a city where UN solders keep a fragile peace after an incursion by Islamic extremists in 2012. Alice relives the city's glorious past with a visit to its world famous mosques, meets the Tuaregs - desert nomads who have sought refuge in there - and celebrates at a party with her newfound friends.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a star-studded version of the general knowledge quiz in which the contestants must try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. In this edition, Jay Aston, Mike Nolan, SuRie, Dana, Jade Ewen, John Lundvik, Niamh Kavanagh and Mans Zelmerlow take part in a special Eurovision edition of the general knowledge quiz. Plus, a special performance from Dana.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision: Come Together
programme.desc: With Eurovision 2020 unable to go ahead as planned, Graham Norton gives viewers the chance to vote for their all-time favourite Eurovision song from an eclectic shortlist, selected by a panel of experts. Graham also chats to James Newman, who was due to represent the UK in Rotterdam this year. There are previews of some of the other most talked-about acts who were due to take to the stage this year, and Eurovision fans and recreate classic moments in their homes. Former UK act Sonia even takes on the Epic Sax Guy solo that became so infamous after the 2010 Eurovision Song Contest.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision: Europe Shine a Light
programme.desc: A special programme honouring all 41 songs that would have competed in the 2020 Eurovision song contest. Just like the contest itself, the live show will be simultaneously broadcast across Europe and beyond, and will be hosted from the Netherlands by Dutch presenters Chantal Janzen, Edsilia Rombley and Jan Smit. There will be a virtual singalong, where fans from across the world will dress up and form a huge Eurovision choir to perform Johnny Logan's winning song for Ireland in 1980, What's Another Year. Graham Norton provides live commentary.(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway walks along a stunning - and often overlooked - stretch of coastline, dominated by the Second Severn Crossing or Prince of Wales Bridge. On his journey, he rings an ancient church bell, finds a water scorpion that breathes through its bottom, learns about the Rogiet Hoard and discovers how the village of Sudbrook evolved. He also wades into the River Severn, following in the footsteps of ancient fisherman, and finishes in Chepstow at the start - or end - of the Wales Coast Path.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth and Joe Swift kick off a week of special programmes celebrating past stagings of the horticultural event by reflecting on a decade of Best Show Garden award winners. Mary Berry champions one of the greatest rose growers in the Great Pavilion, while Arit Anderson looks at the trendiest houseplants and there is also the launch of the BBC RHS People's Choice of the Decade vote, where the public get to vote on their favourite People's Choice winning garden of the past 10 years.(n)

programme.title: Primates
programme.desc: With more than half of the world's primates under threat of extinction, Chris Packham narrates a look at what is being done to protect primates, with scientists making ground-breaking discoveries about this remarkable animal family. Researcher Dr Cat Hobaiter has spent 13 years studying chimpanzees in the jungles of Uganda, while on Thailand's East coast, Dr Amanda Tan reveals how long-tailed macaques use tools to harvest shellfish.(n)

programme.title: The Rugby Codebreakers
programme.desc: Carolyn Hitt presents a look at the Welsh rugby players during the 100-year amateur era who broke what was considered a taboo by many to switch codes from union to league. Carolyn travels to Huddersfield where in 1895, members of 22 teams decided to resign from the amateur English Rugby Union, with the professional rugby league being born as a result. She also provides an insight into those from South Wales who found great success after crossing codes in the 1920s and the black players who flourished in the 1970s, including Roy Francis and Clive Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Educating Rita (1983)
programme.desc: A Liverpool hairdresser unhappy with her marriage and her lot in life enrols on an Open University course hoping to expand her horizons. The lively pupil provides some much-needed inspiration for her boozy, world-weary tutor, but over time, he begins to question whether his influence is entirely positive. Comedy drama based on Willy Russell's stage play, starring Michael Caine and Julie Walters.(n)

programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus Crisis: Europe's Migrant Camps - Panorama
programme.desc: Said Reza Adib investigates conditions inside Greek migrant camps, locked down as coronavirus spread across the world, with vulnerable people havinge to share taps and toilets with those who have tested positive for the virus. Strict quarantine is enforced on camps where coronavirus cases are confirmed, leading to some shortages in food, water and medical care inside. The programme hears from charity and public health experts who warn that any failure to control the spread of the virus inside the camps could lead to potentially dangerous outbreaks.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tina calls an ambulance for Jean and Suki steps in to help - but when she realises all is not as it seems, she tries to manipulate the situation to her favour. Callum and Kathy worry that Ben is unwell, putting his surgery in doubt, while Linda is taken aback when Phil seems more concerned about buying the Vic than his son's operation. Keegan's actions leave Tiffany torn, Isaac opens up to Denise and Ian plays Habiba at her own game.(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen references clips from half a century and more of Welsh television as he sets out on a quest to discover the truth about rugby. It's a story of vodka, Groggs and adventures away, full of lively characters from the nation's sporting past.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Connell bumps into Marianne back in Sligo and despite the awkwardness between them he offers to attend her father's anniversary mass the next day. Back in Dublin, the two study hard for the Trinity Scholarship exams, but Connell is mugged on a night out after the results are announced and he turns to Marianne for help. On arriving at her house, he sees that he has interrupted a get-together, which includes Jamie. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: It is the summer holidays and Connell arrives at Marianne's family house in Italy, but the obvious chemistry between him and Marianne causes friction with Jamie. Peggy cooks a lavish meal, but tensions run high during dinner, with Jamie drinking too much and picking a fight with Marianne.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus Crisis: Europe's Migrant Camps - Panorama
programme.desc: Said Reza Adib investigates conditions inside Greek migrant camps, locked down as coronavirus spread across the world, with vulnerable people havinge to share taps and toilets with those who have tested positive for the virus. Strict quarantine is enforced on camps where coronavirus cases are confirmed, leading to some shortages in food, water and medical care inside. The programme hears from charity and public health experts who warn that any failure to control the spread of the virus inside the camps could lead to potentially dangerous outbreaks.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tina calls an ambulance for Jean and Suki steps in to help - but when she realises all is not as it seems, she tries to manipulate the situation to her favour. Callum and Kathy worry that Ben is unwell, putting his surgery in doubt, while Linda is taken aback when Phil seems more concerned about buying the Vic than his son's operation. Keegan's actions leave Tiffany torn, Isaac opens up to Denise and Ian plays Habiba at her own game.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Greg Davies and Guz Khan, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman and historian Lucy Worsley join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Connell bumps into Marianne back in Sligo and despite the awkwardness between them he offers to attend her father's anniversary mass the next day. Back in Dublin, the two study hard for the Trinity Scholarship exams, but Connell is mugged on a night out after the results are announced and he turns to Marianne for help. On arriving at her house, he sees that he has interrupted a get-together, which includes Jamie. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Normal People

programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours the recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, for unwanted items that she can breathe new life into. A set of wooden bed ends gives Sarah an exciting new challenge.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a star-studded version of the general knowledge quiz in which the contestants must try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. In this edition, Jay Aston, Mike Nolan, SuRie, Dana, Jade Ewen, John Lundvik, Niamh Kavanagh and Mans Zelmerlow take part in a special Eurovision edition of the general knowledge quiz. Plus, a special performance from Dana.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision: Come Together
programme.desc: With Eurovision 2020 unable to go ahead as planned, Graham Norton gives viewers the chance to vote for their all-time favourite Eurovision song from an eclectic shortlist, selected by a panel of experts. Graham also chats to James Newman, who was due to represent the UK in Rotterdam this year. There are previews of some of the other most talked-about acts who were due to take to the stage this year, and Eurovision fans and recreate classic moments in their homes. Former UK act Sonia even takes on the Epic Sax Guy solo that became so infamous after the 2010 Eurovision Song Contest.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision: Europe Shine a Light
programme.desc: A special programme honouring all 41 songs that would have competed in the 2020 Eurovision song contest. Just like the contest itself, the live show will be simultaneously broadcast across Europe and beyond, and will be hosted from the Netherlands by Dutch presenters Chantal Janzen, Edsilia Rombley and Jan Smit. There will be a virtual singalong, where fans from across the world will dress up and form a huge Eurovision choir to perform Johnny Logan's winning song for Ireland in 1980, What's Another Year. Graham Norton provides live commentary.(n)

programme.title: Penguins - Spy in the Huddle
programme.desc: The emperor penguins are swamped by hungry imposters as they try to feed their chicks, the humboldts are left in their burrows as the adults head for a beach swarming with cormorants, and the rockhopper youngsters practise their jumping skills. Eventually, having grown up and become independent, all the penguins leave for the sea, tackling the same hazards as their parents before them. David Tennant narrates.(n)
