programme.desc: Sophie Raworth and Joe Swift kick off a week of special programmes celebrating past stagings of the horticultural event by reflecting on a decade of Best Show Garden award winners. Mary Berry champions one of the greatest rose growers in the Great Pavilion, while Arit Anderson looks at the trendiest houseplants and there is also the launch of the BBC RHS People's Choice of the Decade vote, where the public get to vote on their favourite People's Choice winning garden of the past 10 years.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison is on her home turf in Gloucestershire, where she helps with important conservation work at Slimbridge Wetland Centre, gets a ringside seat to watch some kingfisher parents busy feeding their young and explores the treasures of an ancient bluebell wood. Adam Henson reveals how his horses are helping to create a wildlife haven and Tom Heap investigates how fly-tipping is blighting the countryside more than ever.(n)

programme.title: Primates
programme.desc: With more than half of the world's primates under threat of extinction, Chris Packham narrates a look at what is being done to protect primates, with scientists making ground-breaking discoveries about this remarkable animal family. Researcher Dr Cat Hobaiter has spent 13 years studying chimpanzees in the jungles of Uganda, while on Thailand's East coast, Dr Amanda Tan reveals how long-tailed macaques use tools to harvest shellfish.(n)

programme.desc: A Liverpool hairdresser unhappy with her marriage and her lot in life enrols on an Open University course hoping to expand her horizons. The lively pupil provides some much-needed inspiration for her boozy, world-weary tutor, but over time, he begins to question whether his influence is entirely positive. Comedy drama based on Willy Russell's stage play, starring Michael Caine and Julie Walters.(n)

programme.title: 1964 General Election
programme.desc: A chance to revisit the BBC's coverage of the historic General Election of 1964, which saw Harold Wilson's Labour Party secure a majority of just four seats to form what would eventually turn out to be an unstable and unworkable parliament. Richard Dimbleby presents as live as the results were delivered on Thursday, October 15. Includes analysis by Ian Trethowan, David Butler and Robert McKenzie and interviews by Robin Day, Cliff Michelmore and David Frost.(n)

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: While some countries are claiming success in their effort to curb Covid-19, Brazil is increasingly looking like an outlier. The rate of new infections is still increasing, the death toll is mounting, and all the while President Jair Bolsonaro focuses on easing social distancing and reopening the Brazilian economy. HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to Brazil's former Health Minister Luiz Henrique Mandetta who was fired by Mr Bolsonaro after a series of disagreements. Is President Bolsonaro putting his nation at risk?(n)

programme.title: Call air Cladach Ile (The Loss on Islay's Shore)
programme.desc: The story of two sea tragedies that brought the First World War to the Hebrides. In February 1918, the troopship Tuscania was torpedoed by a German U-boat. More than 200 American soldiers and British crew died as lifeboats were dashed against Islay's cliffs. Eight months later, HMS Otranto collided with another ship in its convoy in a fierce storm, killing nearly 500 men. Just 19 reached Islay's shore alive.(n)

programme.desc: Andrew Bruce from Far North is busy at Wick Airport, where three ferry pilots have come in for fuel on their way to Africa, Holland and America. One of them has broken down and its front wheel needs to be repaired and with only a permit to fly in daylight, time is running out. At Aberdeen Airport, the pilots of the Air Ambulance plane travel to Stornoway and Kirkwall, while a private plane arrives in Benbecula with eight passengers who are heading to the famous golf course in Askernish, South Uist.(n)

This film offers a poignant, moving and though provoking account of the impact of loneliness in the modern world.(n)

Chief Inspector Maigret comes under huge pressure from the public and his superiors to apprehend the killer before he can strike again. Fiona Shaw and Mark Heap head up the supporting cast.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden

programme.desc: A wily thief gathers a band of crooks for a plot to break into a maternity hospital and steal the supply of contraceptive pills. One of the gang gets the lie of the land by disguising himself as a nurse, but the hypochondriac head doctor and formidable matron prove constant obstacles to the heist. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams and Hattie Jacques.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A highly respected scientist is murdered just before he is about to deliver a controversial lecture on the environment. It becomes clear he had many enemies within the profession, but Morse's investigations lead him to a killer who is altogether closer to home. Mystery, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Geoffrey Palmer, Cheryl Campbell and George Costigan.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately as the senior detective with Laurence Fox as his sidekick Hathaway.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bodies of a glamorous senior citizen and a distinguished doctor are found beside a river - and although suspicion for their murders initially falls on poachers, Barnaby suspects a more monetary motive for the attack. However, the case becomes complicated with the discovery that a local restaurateur, who had recently learnt his wife had been having an affair with the male victim, has also met an untimely end. With Honor Blackman, Philip Franks, James Hazeldine and Jeff Rawle.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. Following a trail of clues to London, Marple is drawn to the Pale Horse Inn - where she discovers a sinister world of occult practices. Drama, starring Julia McKenzie in the title role, with Lynda Baron, Pauline Collins, Nicholas Parsons and Neil Pearson.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin spend an evening at home alone - and the absence of a chaperone allows the trainee chef's amorous side to surface. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve and Leanne view a nursery school for Oliver, but matters take a dramatic turn on their return home when Oliver suffers a seizure. Gail tells Sarah and David she expects them at her birthday soirée at Number 8, and Cathy threatens to quit her job.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David encounters some drug dealers on a rough estate, but when he jumps on the bonnet of their car, they give chase, putting him in danger. Steve and Leanne describe Oliver's symptoms to a doctor and Cathy and Bernie call a truce at the kebab shop.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Oliver suffers another frightening seizure and is moved to intensive care, much to Steve and Leanne's horror. Ed and Michael experience racism at work, while Gemma attends a support group for her depression and takes comfort from realising other people are in the same position as her.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel cuts Dotty out of the burning tent, and as the toddler is taken to the doctor's surgery, Archie and Arthur find themselves in serious trouble, while Jai demands an explanation. Malone tells a furious Harriet that he has finished his dealings with Billy, Cain and Will, and Leyla encourages a dispirited Andrea to use the family holiday as a chance to remind Jamie why he married her.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Arthur is confused when he comes downstairs to find PC Swirling at the front door, while Rhona brings over Johnny's guardianship documents, leaving Charity hurt. Will apologises to Harriet, and Wendy continues to seem blind to Bob's feelings for her.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Malone and Harriet have another encounter, while Al is delighted when Priya reveals she is ready to go public with their relationship, but their plans are cut short when Jai walks in on them. Cain and Moira meet to finalise their divorce, and Leanna relishes wrecking her dad's relationship.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Egyptian Tomb
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Up the Jungle (1970)
programme.desc: A Victorian explorer recalls how he was recruited by an aristocrat to find her missing son in the African jungle. Teaming up with a fearless hunter, he hoped to find a rare bird on his journey - but instead fell into the clutches of an all-female tribe. Comedy, starring Sid James, Frankie Howerd, Joan Sims, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Terry Scott.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A land dispute escalates during a cowboy fair run by the Wild West Society in the village of Ford Florey. Farmers Jack Fincher and Silas Burbage both claim to own a patch of wasteland known as the Swamp, but Dan Malko wants to buy it and create a travellers' site. Barnaby and Jones are called in when the feud leads to murder during the Wild West-style shoot-outs at the annual event. Guest starring Kenneth Cranham, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Sir Cliff Richard: 60 Years in Public and in Private
programme.desc: A profile of the singer filmed over a six-month period at his home in Portugal, at Abbey Road Studios and at Wimbledon, examining his career highs and lows and his record-breaking longevity. Cliff talks about the dark days that followed the police raid on his home in 2014, his battle to clear his name and the people whose support have got him through tough times. Featuring interviews with Barry Gibb, Olivia Newton-John, Hank Marvin, Jimmy Osmond, Andrew Lloyd Webber, Brian May and Joan Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Nation's Favourite Carpenters Song
programme.desc: Richard Carpenter takes a seat at the piano to celebrate some of the musical hits he achieved alongside his sister Karen between 1969 and 1983. Over the course of 13 years, the California-born pair recorded 11 albums, 31 singles, and a selection of TV specials, and hit the top of the US charts with three number-one releases. However, their career was cut short by Karen's death in 1983, as the result of heart failure brought on her eating disorder. This programme charts the highs and devastating lows of the brother-sister duo's remarkable time in the limelight, and makes use of a new ITV poll revealing which Carpenters' song has been voted the UK's favourite. Features contributions by the Carpenters' friends and collaborators, including Burt Bacharach and Petula Clark, and famous fans such as Nicole Scherzinger, the Corrs, and Myleene Klass.(n)

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: West Ham player Bobby leads England to World Cup glory at Wembley and he and Tina are reunited amidst the raucous celebrations. Fame and fortune follow as the couple become icons of their time, and when baby Dean arrives Tina thinks she has it all. The price of celebrity soon becomes apparent when Tina is besieged after Bobby is arrested in Colombia accused of theft, and although he goes on to play well at the 1970 Mexico World Cup, they return home to a kidnap threat and a wedge increasingly being driven between them. Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Spanish Chest
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: The residents visit an auction to buy a giraffe after their own is killed by lions, but when Danny and Alice argue over a suitable replacement, he is led into the arms of Vanessa. Meanwhile, an approaching storm means the family has to release some of the animals for safety reasons - but when it hits, Charlotte and Danny come off worst. Stephen Tompkinson, Dawn Steele and Mary-Anne Barlow star.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel cuts Dotty out of the burning tent, and as the toddler is taken to the doctor's surgery, Archie and Arthur find themselves in serious trouble, while Jai demands an explanation. Malone tells a furious Harriet that he has finished his dealings with Billy, Cain and Will, and Leyla encourages a dispirited Andrea to use the family holiday as a chance to remind Jamie why he married her.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Malone and Harriet have another encounter, while Al is delighted when Priya reveals she is ready to go public with their relationship, but their plans are cut short when Jai walks in on them. Cain and Moira meet to finalise their divorce, and Leanna relishes wrecking her dad's relationship.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack has second thoughts about buying the shop. Liz is delighted when Steve phones to say he and Vicky are married. Reg is stunned when Eric makes a big announcement.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack decides to throw a party for his neighbours. Percy commemorates VJ Day in the Rovers. Reg attempts to convince the Duckworths to think again about buying the shop.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police arrive at the Windsors to find Scott still missing. Rachel gives Chris an ultimatum. Nick tells Pete the whole story about Kim's threat to Alice.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is set on revenge and Rachel reaches the end of the line with Chris.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Eileen Needham sues for divorce after a domestic dispute gets out of hand, and her hard-nosed solicitor Sylvia Swinton (played by Outnumbered actress Samantha Bond) does little to calm the situation, so Joe steps in to play the peacemaker. Stan Bickle arrives in Aidensfield, bringing his daughter Josie (guest star Jenna Boyd), whose flirtatious ways steal David's heart and bring out the worst in Peggy.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: Climb the Greasy Pole - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. A seat becomes vacant on the local council when Sam Popham is taken ill, so Pop stands in the election, and Mariette returns from holiday with Charley. David Jason stars.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: A housing shortage forces a compromise between the flatmates and their disapproving landlord. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted).(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar's golfing pal asks him to investigate the disappearance of his son, who left home after a blazing row with his overbearing father and has been spotted frequenting a club in Whitby. Meanwhile, the Aidensfield police officers are baffled by the activities of a modern-day Robin Hood, who has committed a string of burglaries and given away his ill-gotten gains to the elderly residents of a new block of flats. Drama, starring Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Passions run high as Frank finally confronts Kim and Dave.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim attempts to convince Frank that the baby is his and not Dave's. Frank spends the night at Zoe's and feels like an old fool. Mandy tries to flirt with Dave in the tearoom.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Father Donnelly breaks some tragic news to Don. Curly, Kevin, Jim, Andy and Des form a five-a-side football team. Jack has some fun looking at expensive cars.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera is shocked by Ivy's death. Curly signs his team up to a five-a-side league. Nick receives a letter from Ivy in the event of her death which contains some stunning news.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin comes home with a puppy, to Chrissy's dismay. Flat-share comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan, Sally Thomsett and Paula Wilcox.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Missing Will
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar's golfing pal asks him to investigate the disappearance of his son, who left home after a blazing row with his overbearing father and has been spotted frequenting a club in Whitby. Meanwhile, the Aidensfield police officers are baffled by the activities of a modern-day Robin Hood, who has committed a string of burglaries and given away his ill-gotten gains to the elderly residents of a new block of flats. Drama, starring Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: First episode of the costume drama set in an Edwardian country house in 1912, written by Oscar-winning writer Julian Fellowes. The lives of the Crawley family and Downton Abbey's servants are changed for ever when the sinking of the Titanic leaves the estate without its heir and his son. The arrival of a new valet shocks the staff, who scheme to make his stay as short-lived as possible. Starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The newly promoted Detective Sergeant Morse investigates when a famous international thief tries to steal a Faberge egg from an auction. The case leads to the gruesome murders of an academic and a gangster, and Morse is convinced the deaths are the work of the same man. As he struggles with his new responsibilities, he finds himself forced to mentor a younger partner (played by Poldark's Lewis Peek).(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Promoted to Detective Chief Superintendent, Bancroft has professional success but at great personal cost. When a double murder brings an unexpected reunion, Elizabeth is forced onto the sidelines by Detective Superintendent Jake Harper. However, as new enemies emerge, old foes make their move. Thriller, starring Sarah Parish, David Avery, Charlotte Hope, Adam Long and Lee Boardman.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The rent money disappears, forcing Jo to consider selling a prized possession. Flat-share comedy, with Sally Thomsett, Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team come to the aid of nonagenarian Roberta and her 86-year-old best friend and carer Enid, who live together in a retirement bungalow in Wiveliscombe, Somerset. Originally from Liverpool, the two plucky pensioners have helped each other out countless times throughout the course of their friendship, which has lasted seven decades. However, they now find themselves trapped in their home by a treacherous slope in their garden, so Alan plans to set them free by designing a future-proofed garden in a vintage style.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Dorset creating an exciting garden for a young family. His idea for the Drakes is to give them an outdoor space that reflects terminally ill dad Andrew's love of the great outdoors and his passion for working with wood. He opts for a garden with a frontier-land theme that Andrew, wife Rachel and their young son Lucas can enjoy, creating a prairie-inspired back yard of big scale rustic wood structures, agricultural motifs with craftsmanship and the spirit of adventure at its core.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve and Leanne view a nursery school for Oliver, but matters take a dramatic turn on their return home when Oliver suffers a seizure. Gail tells Sarah and David she expects them at her birthday soirée at Number 8, and Cathy threatens to quit her job.(n)

programme.desc: Oliver suffers another frightening seizure and is moved to intensive care, much to Steve and Leanne's horror. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star.(n)

The price of celebrity soon becomes apparent when Tina is besieged after Bobby is arrested in Colombia accused of theft, and although he goes on to play well at the 1970 Mexico World Cup, they return home to a kidnap threat and a wedge increasingly being driven between them. Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Spanish Chest

programme.title: Wild at Heart

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

How have they been getting on since the cameras stopped rolling?(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner get caught up in a dispute between a landlord and a tenant who refuses to move out, and Brian O'Shaughnessy and Delroy Anglin chase a £17,000 debt. In Essex, a couple owe their neighbour £11,000, and on the south coast, debt collection from a Turkish restaurant gets complicated when there is confusion about ownership.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The debt collection agents grapple with emotional evictions and moral dilemmas during the festive period, including a young family in Crystal Palace who are made homeless when the landlord repossesses their home because he wants to redevelop the property. The eviction comes as a shock to the family as they are up to date with their rent, but have just one hour to gather their belongings. Plus, an acrimonious divorce in Chessington comes to a head when the estranged wife obtains a writ to reclaim the house, and an off-licence owner in Hampshire with unpaid rent of more than £5,000 battles to keep his business open just as everything shuts down for the holidays.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Elmore are met with lies and aggression when they track down nearly £3,000 owed by a garage mechanic, while Steve and Ben encounter a retired couple who are being forced to leave their home. Stuart and Vic revisit a young debtor who owe £750 for a laptop computer, and in Oxfordshire, Paul and Steve chase £30,000 owed by a world-famous Formula One racing team.(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Susan is stressed to breaking point and when Mackenzie asks her for advice at the wrong moment, she explodes. Terese tries to smooth things over between Paul and Harlow.(n)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Marco blackmails Maggie into persuading Ben to invest in his business, while Jasmine asks Irene not to tell anyone about her pregnancy until she has told Tori.(n)

programme.title: Now That's Funny!

programme.sub-title: On Benefits: To Heat or to Eat?
programme.desc: A single father with six children cannot afford to both feed his family and heat their home, while a man who was seriously injured in a car accident more than 10 years ago struggles to prove to the authorities that he is unable to work, and a woman setting up a fortune telling business faces an uncertain future.(n)

programme.desc: An old friend of Jessica's dies and the heir to a fortune, who has been missing for more than 20 years, makes a sudden reappearance claiming to have had amnesia. Angela Lansbury stars as the sleuthing crime writer.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.desc: One of Jessica's former students publishes a best-selling novel, much to the excitement of the citizens of Cabot Cove - but their delight turns to dismay as fiction begins to mirror reality. Murder mystery, starring Angela Lansbury.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is delighted to run into an old friend while on a shopping expedition in Boston - until he unaccountably denies knowing her. She recruits her dependable ally Harry McGraw to help solve the mystery. Jerry Orbach guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica takes time out from writing to visit her nephew - but soon has to put on her thinking cap to solve the murder of a TV sports personality. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury and Paul Sorvino.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Fisher Stevens and Molly Hagan.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Grand Deceptions (1989)
programme.desc: An army colonel who embezzled money from his superior officer to fund arms sales becomes the target of a blackmail attempt when his crimes are discovered. The crooked officer kills the extortionist and makes it look like an accident, but the crumpled cop is soon on his case. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Foxworth and Janet Elber.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Suitable for Framing (1971)
programme.desc: A millionaire art collector is thought to have been killed as the result of a random burglary. However, Columbo is convinced the dead man's nephew committed the crime to avoid being written out of the will, and embarks on a mission to ensnare his suspect. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Ross Martin and Don Ameche.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Playback (1975)
programme.desc: An inventor tries to stop his mother-in-law from exposing his infidelities by murdering her in front of a security camera - and tampering with the recording to make it look like the killing occurred at a time when he had an alibi. However, Columbo sees through his hi-tech ruse and tries to expose the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Oskar Werner and Gena Rowlands.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: The Greenhouse Jungle (1972)
programme.desc: A penniless rare-plant collector, desperate for funds to support his expensive hobby, fakes his nephew's kidnapping to get his hands on a trust fund. However, the supposed victim turns up dead, prompting Columbo to take action to unmask the killer. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Bradford Dillman and Ray Milland.(n)

programme.title: Columbo Goes to College (1991)
programme.desc: Two criminology students murder their professor and cover their tracks with false clues. They think they are on the way to outwitting everyone - but find themselves at the bottom of the class when Columbo intervenes. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Stephen Caffrey, Gary Hershberger and Robert Culp.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: The Most Dangerous Match (1973)
programme.desc: An American chess champion fears he is about to be beaten by a superior player from Russia, and decides the only sure-fire way he can avoid defeat is by murdering his opponent. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Laurence Harvey and Jack Kruschen.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: The murder of a dentist leads Goren and Eames to a company at the heart of a criminal conspiracy involving contaminated mouthwash that has caused the deaths of several children. Establishing a motive for the killing proves difficult - and the detectives begin to suspect they are being manipulated by someone close to the investigation. Drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler tries to rescue a youngster who has been kidnapped by a man with schizophrenia, but resorts to unorthodox tactics that jeopardise his own career. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A man is arrested for assaulting his alcoholic wife, who is pregnant - but changes the focus of the case by trying to have her charged with neglect for drinking while expecting a baby. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The bomb squad is called out to deal with a suspicious suitcase found on a school bus - but it proves to contain the body of an eight-year-old beauty pageant princess. Charles dresses in full Highland regalia to hear how Sir Walter Scott reinvented tartan, while James discovers the story behind one of the most intriguing incidents of the Second World War. James believes silver antiques will steal him victory, but Charles plumps for a little porcelain King Charles Spaniel hoping for big profits.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts explore a house in Devon, a property in Donnington, Shropshire, and a semi-detached home in Brandon, Co Durham, then follow the sales and talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings. After three months the team returns to review their progress.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts take a look at a cottage in Cornwall, a flat in Shepherd's Bush, west London, and a former jeweller's shop in Denton, Greater Manchester, all of which have been sold at auction.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi heads to Ramsbottom in Greater Manchester to lend her expertise to Charlotte Belcher, who has been running the 14-bedroom Grant Arms Hotel for two and a half years. Despite its attractive, central location, the 19th-century listed venue has developed a bad reputation as a hang-out for a 'rough crowd' - particularly at the bar. With no profits to pay for renovations, Charlotte has enlisted her husband and two children to help turn the Grant Arms around, but her work-life balance is starting to tip drastically away from home, and the beleaguered hotelier hopes Alex will find a way to boost the hotel's income while also restoring her private life.(n)

programme.title: Embarrassing Bodies
programme.desc: Pixie McKenna investigates new surgery techniques involving the anus, and helps a man suffering with hot testicles. Dawn Harper meets a woman who had a stroke at just 45, revealing whether electrotherapy can help her regain more of her bodily functions, and investigates the worrying trend of buying legal highs online. Christian Jessen is joined by Nurse Joy Tickle to help treat assorted skin conditions for visitors in Birmingham, and in Magaluf there's a look at the serious side effects of spiking someone's drink.(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family Australia

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Tjure's hammer is struck by lightning and gets stuck in a stone in between Flake and a neighbouring village - but both communities claim this is a blessing from the thunder god.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Ylvi is captured by the pirates and forced to work as a maid on their ship - but she causes her captors no end of trouble.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The energetic zebra encourages his friends to find a hobby they enjoy.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: McMeer is upset when Zigby loses his special hat.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Sven and Halvar work together to escape from a giant and steal his treasure - but the pirate captain tries to take all the loot for himself.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: The crew explores a tunnel full of traps - and Ulme's musical talents may be the key to everyone getting out in one piece.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra tries to write a new story for Zara and Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra plans a fancy-dress contest, but McMeer has trouble finding a costume.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Susan decides to go back to her husband, while Ash gets some help from the Navy in learning hoe to dispose of new German land mines.(n)

programme.title: Meet the Lords
programme.desc: Documentary filmed over a year following life at the House of Lords, one of Britain's oldest, most idiosyncratic and most important institutions, going behind the scenes during a turbulent 12 months that has seen dramatic changes in the political landscape and the Lords battling it out with the Government. The first edition introduces the larger-than-life characters that populate the House, including new people's peers, hereditaries and political grandees, as they try to change legislation and follow their personal passions.(n)

programme.title: Dear Murderer (1947)
programme.desc: A man believes he has committed the perfect crime when he kills his wife's two lovers in a jealous rage. Melodrama, starring Eric Portman, Dennis Price, Jack Warner, Greta Gynt and Hazel Court.(n)

programme.title: Victim (1961)
programme.desc: A secretly gay barrister discovers his lover has been driven to suicide by blackmailers, who have also extorted money from several other men. Risking his reputation and marriage, he hunts down the culprits, fully aware that it could lead to his own exposure as a homosexual, illegal at the time. Drama, starring Dirk Bogarde, Sylvia Syms, Dennis Price and Anthony Nicholls.(n)

programme.title: All Coppers Are (1971)
programme.desc: A young policeman and a small-time crook are both involved with the same girl. Drama, starring Martin Potter, Julia Foster and David Essex.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Canary Wharf and Southbank. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Homeowners work to increase the value of their properties, redesigning and refurbishing for maximum profits.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Ash asks Susan to marry him as the team heads for the coast in preparation for D-Day.(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: The doctors are in Glasgow where they discover whether olive oil is healthy using a cutting edge technique. Meanwhile, Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates whether beards are unhygienic, Michael Mosley finds out about meat, and Dr Saleyha Ahsan hears from survivors of sepsis about the warning signs of the little-known condition.(n)

programme.title: Oxford Street Revealed
programme.desc: Observational documentary revealing the inner workings of Oxford Street, following police and paramedics as they keep the public safe, as well as the staff in the shops and on the underground.(n)

programme.title: The Repo Man
programme.desc: In recession-hit Britain, many people have turned to finance companies to purchase items over a fixed period of time. This documentary follows the life and work of bailiff Sean James as he confronts debtors across the nation who fail to keep up their repayments.(n)

programme.title: Operation Chromite (2016)
programme.desc: A squad of South Korean soldiers sent to infiltrate their northern neighbours during the crucial Battle of Incheon. Wartime drama, starring Lee Jung-jae and Liam Neeson.(n)

programme.title: One Night in Powder (2015)
programme.desc: A clueless music promoter hits the streets of East London, hoping to put together the greatest club night the capital has ever seen, and recruiting the most eccentric residents of Dalston to help him. Documentary-style comedy, starring Jason Attar.(n)

programme.title: The Repo Man
programme.desc: In recession-hit Britain, many people have turned to finance companies to purchase items over a fixed period of time. This documentary follows the life and work of bailiff Sean James as he confronts debtors across the nation who fail to keep up their repayments.(n)

programme.title: Dying of the Light (2014)
programme.desc: A CIA agent is diagnosed with a degenerative condition that results in him being forced to retire from the world of intelligence. When he receives word that a terrorist who once tortured him has resurfaced, he goes rogue and sets out to assassinate the man while he is still able. Thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, Anton Yelchin and Irene Jacob.(n)

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary goes to the pub to drown his sorrows, so Dermot invites Deborah to their flat for a romantic evening of candlelight, dinner and music. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes.(n)

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The Rolling Stones guitarist is joined by Stereophonics frontman Kelly Jones, who discusses some of his favourite songs, including Otis Redding's Cigarettes and Coffee and Who Killed Davey Moore? by Bob Dylan.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Canary Wharf and Southbank. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: The Salvage Gang (1958)
programme.desc: A group of children breaks a saw, so takes on a series of odd jobs to replace it before anyone notices. Family drama, starring Christopher Warbey and Frazer Hines.(n)

programme.title: The Captain's Table (1959)
programme.desc: A gruff cargo skipper is given the command of a luxurious liner, but finds it difficult to mind his manners among the upper-class passengers. Comedy, with John Gregson, Donald Sinden, Peggy Cummins and Reginald Beckwith.(n)

programme.title: The Big Money (1956)
programme.desc: An incompetent thief finally manages to make his family of crooks proud of him by pulling off a major robbery, but finds his ill-gotten gains bring him anything but happiness. Comedy, starring Ian Carmichael, Belinda Lee, Kathleen Harrison and Robert Helpmann.(n)

programme.title: Trio (1950)
programme.desc: Adaptation of three short stories by W Somerset Maugham, The Verger, Mr Know-Al, and The Sanatorium. Starring James Hayter, Anne Crawford and Jean Simmons.(n)

programme.title: Too Many Crooks (1959)
programme.desc: Bungling crooks kidnap a millionaire's wife, but he is glad to be rid of her and refuses to pay the ransom - so she gets her own back by helping them steal all his cash instead. Comedy, starring Terry-Thomas, George Cole, Sid James, Brenda De Banzie and Bernard Bresslaw.(n)

programme.title: Brief Encounter (1974)
programme.desc: Two married strangers meet by chance in a railway station waiting room, and their paths later cross many times until the pair become friends and their meetings a fixed arrangement. But as their feelings for each other transform from friendship into passion, both come to realise the affair is doomed to fail. Remake of David Lean's 1948 classic, starring Richard Burton and Sophia Loren.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.sub-title: Forest Hill, Hither Green, Cubitt Town
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Forest Hill, Hither Green and Cubitt Town. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The guitarist is joined by photographer and former Mary Quant model Pattie Boyd, who discusses her life in rock music and shares some of her favourite records.(n)

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny visits Patrick and Vanessa from Aberdare in South Wales, who have re-mortgaged their home and bought two dilapidated cottages they have not even seen for £25,000.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a home abroad.(n)

programme.title: Capital Conversation
programme.desc: Author and entrepreneur Michael Hayman interviews the business leaders at the heart of London's most exciting success stories.(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: The doctors reveal the secrets to sticking to health resolutions made at New Year, with surgeon Gabriel Weston experimenting with a herbal supplement said to help people drink less alcohol. Dr Chris van Tulleken reveals unusual ways of getting a better night's sleep, and Michael Mosley discovers how to stay motivated. Meanwhile, Dr Saleyha Ahsan tests some revolutionary new science that could help people easily lose weight and be healthier.(n)

programme.title: Callan
programme.sub-title: The Good Ones Are All Dead
programme.desc: First episode of the spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent. Callan is begrudgingly assigned to protect a Nazi war criminal living in hiding.(n)

programme.title: One Crazy Thing (2016)
programme.desc: An actor's career collapses after a series of mistakes, and ends up an online laughing stock when his ex-girlfriend leaks their sex tape. He finds new hope in a relationship with a woman who has turned her back on the internet - leaving him wondering how or even if to tell her about the embarrassing footage. Romantic comedy, starring Ray Panthaki and Daisy Bevan.(n)

programme.title: Cries from Syria (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary about crimes against humanity in Syria, featuring first-hand accounts from activists, child protesters and a former army general.(n)

programme.title: Rude Boy (1980)
programme.desc: A fan of new wave punks The Clash follows his musical heroes on tour, but slowly finds himself disagreeing with their ideals. Ray Gange stars in this semi-documentary view of street politics in the 1980s, including concert footage of the band.(n)

Meanwhile, Dr Saleyha Ahsan tests some revolutionary new science that could help people easily lose weight and be healthier.(n)

programme.title: All Coppers Are (1971)
programme.desc: A young policeman and a small-time crook are both involved with the same girl. Drama, starring Martin Potter, Julia Foster and David Essex.(n)

programme.title: Death Drums Along the River (1963)
programme.desc: A British inspector is called to Africa to investigate the murder of a fellow policeman and uncovers a diamond-smuggling operation seemingly connected to an upriver hospital. Mystery, based on Edgar Wallace's novel Sanders on the River, starring Richard Todd, Marianne Koch, Albert Lieven and Walter Rilla.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Wild Coast. Property experts help British househunters find their dream properties abroad. (2009).(n)

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny meets Lisa, who wants to swap her boring IT job for property development. Having bought a small three-bedroom house in Macclesfield, she sets about transforming it from a bland beige box to something that, thanks to her unique design ideas, will really stand out from the crowd. But are rose petal and leaf skeletons stuck to the wall really going to appeal to potential buyers?(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: An injured Ash is rushed to hospital, his confidence shattered - but an emergency forces him to return to duty. Final episode of the Second World war drama.(n)

programme.title: Oxford Street Revealed
programme.desc: Observational documentary revealing the inner workings of Oxford Street, following police and paramedics as they keep the public safe, as well as the staff in the shops and on the underground.(n)

programme.title: Spitfire Women
programme.desc: The story of the 168 female pilots who served in Britain's Air Transport Auxiliary - an organisation responsible for delivering aircraft to the RAF on the front lines of battle during the Second World War. Despite having no radio or navigation equipment, ATA pilots were expected to fly any kind of aircraft to the destinations where they were most needed, often risking their lives in the process. The film also features interviews with the last surviving female veterans, who speak about the dangers they encountered in the skies and the discrimination they faced back home.(n)

programme.title: Run
programme.desc: A Chinese woman who sells pirated films and stolen phones on the streets of Brixton, south London, has to repay her debts to a local gang, but when an immigration raid leaves her with no home, money or friends, she finds refuge with a barber - and an unlikely bond soon forms. The second of four dramas telling the stories of seemingly unconnected people facing life-changing decisions, starring Katie Leung, Gershwyn Eustache Jr, Benedict Wong and Lennie James.(n)

programme.title: Warrior Challenge
programme.desc: MMA & K1 from the Troxy in London title fight Mihai Zmarandescu vs Alfie Hawes plus Erol Raif vs George Zorilov, Kat Freeman vs Kat Twardowska.(n)

programme.title: Silk
programme.desc: Tensions run high when Martha represents a mother accused of helping her paralysed teenage daughter to die - and with Clive taking on the prosecution, what begins as a highly contentious court case threatens to spill over into the barristers' personal lives and threaten their relationship. Meanwhile, the resignation of chambers head Alan provides an opportunity for one of the QCs to step up, and Billy's position becomes even more compromised when one of his actions is misinterpreted by a new member of staff. Legal drama, with guest appearances by Claire Skinner (Outnumbered) and Meera Syal (The Kumars).(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner.(n)

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)
